猜想.摘要: Across this run, the evidence chain converges on neutral aromatic as the best-supported mechanism label. Internal structure-based evidence shows an all-hydrocarbon, highly aromatic, compact, and highly planarized organization with no donor-acceptor partitioning and no ESIPT preconditions. Direct torsion screening kept TICT alive as a structural possibility, but the later molecule-specific planarity/compactness screen shifted the pairwise balance toward a more rigid aromatic-core interpretation. The verifier supplement then added close-family precedent indicating that comparable hydrocarbon/polyaryl AIE systems commonly fall into RIR/neutral-aromatic mechanism space and that TICT usually requires stronger charge-separation prerequisites than this run supports. Taken together, the evidence more strongly supports neutral aromatic than TICT, while ICT, ESIPT, and unknown are all materially less plausible.

最终证据摘要: Top1 is neutral aromatic (0.69) and top2 is TICT (0.18). I interpret the verifier evidence as mixed in topic coverage but net-supportive of neutral aromatic for this case: it contributes close-family precedent that all-hydrocarbon polyaryl AIE systems often fit restriction-of-intramolecular-rotation / neutral-aromatic mechanism space, while also indicating that TICT is usually tied to stronger donor-acceptor and charge-separation prerequisites than are evidenced here. This verifier evidence does reduce no remaining portfolio coverage debt because coverage debt was already empty, but it does support pairwise contraction by specifically weakening the main challenger rather than only adding generic context. No still-credible hypothesis remains untested; TICT has been directly screened, unknown has been directly screened by verifier precedent, ICT is blocked and already weak, and ESIPT is dropped with reason. The case therefore legitimately remains in pairwise contraction rather than portfolio screening. Closure justification is now sufficient because the current pair has both a completed molecule-specific internal discriminator and a sufficient close-family verifier supplement that coherently reinforce the same direction. With 14 rounds remaining including the current round, the budget is ample and does not force a premature close; instead it permits a decisive finalize now because the decisive conditions are met.

Across this run, the evidence chain converges on neutral aromatic as the best-supported mechanism label. Internal structure-based evidence shows an all-hydrocarbon, highly aromatic, compact, and highly planarized organization with no donor-acceptor partitioning and no ESIPT preconditions. Direct torsion screening kept TICT alive as a structural possibility, but the later molecule-specific planarity/compactness screen shifted the pairwise balance toward a more rigid aromatic-core interpretation. The verifier supplement then added close-family precedent indicating that comparable hydrocarbon/polyaryl AIE systems commonly fall into RIR/neutral-aromatic mechanism space and that TICT usually requires stronger charge-separation prerequisites than this run supports. Taken together, the evidence more strongly supports neutral aromatic than TICT, while ICT, ESIPT, and unknown are all materially less plausible.

Verifier retrieval is sufficient and close-family specific for neutral aromatic versus TICT. It adds external precedent for hydrocarbon/polyaryl AIE behavior in neutral-aromatic/RIR space while also contextualizing TICT as less favored without stronger donor-acceptor style prerequisites.

Closure justification is sufficient because the current pair now has both molecule-specific internal discrimination and sufficient close-family verifier support pointing in the same net direction. The remaining uncertainty is residual rather than blocking.

Portfolio screening remains complete in round 7 of 20. The verifier evidence does not reopen any portfolio debt; instead it supports pairwise contraction by strengthening the interpretation that the molecule belongs to known hydrocarbon AIE mechanism space and leaving TICT as the only material challenger.

Capabilities were sufficient for decisive closure in this case. Macro delivered the key molecule-specific geometry discriminator successfully, verifier delivered high-information close-family pairwise context, and no additional internal route is required. The earlier microscopic failure was operational and did not block eventual closure because the needed discriminator was obtainable through an exact macro capability.

Key evidence now consists of: internal macro geometry-proxy results showing high planarity_proxy (0.968844), high compactness_proxy (0.957409), aromatic_ring_count 12, hetero_atom_count 0, donor_acceptor_partition_proxy 0.0, rotatable_bond_count 10, and branch_point_count 25; earlier direct rotor/torsion screening showing TICT structural permissiveness but not dominance; and verifier close-family evidence covering AIE/RIR in all-hydrocarbon polyaryl systems plus TICT prerequisite context. The net synthesis favors neutral aromatic over TICT.
